speaker: MOROCCO
text: Mislike me not for my complexion,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: The shadowed livery of the burnished sun,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To whom I am a neighbor and near bred.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Bring me the fairest creature northward born,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Where Phoebus’ fire scarce thaws the icicles,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: And let us make incision for your love

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To prove whose blood is reddest, his or mine.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: I tell thee, lady, this aspect of mine

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Hath feared the valiant; by my love I swear

speaker: MOROCCO
text: The best regarded virgins of our clime

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Have loved it too. I would not change this hue

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Except to steal your thoughts, my gentle queen.

speaker: PORTIA
text: In terms of choice I am not solely led

speaker: PORTIA
text: By nice direction of a maiden’s eyes;

speaker: PORTIA
text: Besides, the lott’ry of my destiny

speaker: PORTIA
text: Bars me the right of voluntary choosing.

speaker: PORTIA
text: But if my father had not scanted me

speaker: PORTIA
text: And hedged me by his wit to yield myself

speaker: PORTIA
text: His wife who wins me by that means I told you,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Yourself, renownèd prince, then stood as fair

speaker: PORTIA
text: As any comer I have looked on yet

speaker: PORTIA
text: For my affection.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Even for that I thank you.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Therefore I pray you lead me to the caskets

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To try my fortune. By this scimitar

speaker: MOROCCO
text: That slew the Sophy and a Persian prince,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: That won three fields of Sultan Solyman,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: I would o’erstare the sternest eyes that look,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Outbrave the heart most daring on the Earth,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Pluck the young sucking cubs from the she-bear,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Yea, mock the lion when he roars for prey,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To win thee, lady. But, alas the while!

speaker: MOROCCO
text: If Hercules and Lychas play at dice

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Which is the better man, the greater throw

speaker: MOROCCO
text: May turn by fortune from the weaker hand;

speaker: MOROCCO
text: So is Alcides beaten by his page,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: And so may I, blind Fortune leading me,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Miss that which one unworthier may attain,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: And die with grieving.

speaker: PORTIA
text: You must take your chance

speaker: PORTIA
text: And either not attempt to choose at all

speaker: PORTIA
text: Or swear before you choose, if you choose wrong

speaker: PORTIA
text: Never to speak to lady afterward

speaker: PORTIA
text: In way of marriage. Therefore be advised.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Nor will not. Come, bring me unto my chance.

speaker: PORTIA
text: First, forward to the temple. After dinner

speaker: PORTIA
text: Your hazard shall be made.

speaker: MOROCCO
text: Good fortune then,

speaker: MOROCCO
text: To make me blest—or cursed’st among men!
